title: carex paupercula var. irrigua
intro: Carex paupercula var. irrigua, also known as the slender wood sedge, is a type of plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The health benefits of Carex paupercula var. irrigua include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or topical creams, and are often used in folk medicine to support overall well-being.
